product_overview: IGreen NPK is a broad-spectrum microbial consortium biofertilizer designed to improve nitrogen, phosphorus, and potassium availability in the rhizosphere. It is suited to integrated nutrient programs where fertilizer-use efficiency and soil biological activity need reinforcement.
key_product_highlights: Multi-functional consortium for N fixation and P/K mobilization
Improves rhizosphere nutrient cycling and root-zone activity
Supports better fertilizer-use efficiency across major nutrients
Useful as a basal and early vegetative biofertility input

mechanism_of_action.description: The microbial consortium colonizes the rhizosphere and root surface, fixes atmospheric nitrogen through associative N-fixing members, solubilizes bound phosphorus and mobilizes native potassium pools, and drives microbial nutrient cycling in the soil. This combined activity improves nutrient availability, root uptake efficiency, and overall crop nutrition.

dilution_dosage_per_acre.crop_stage: Basal soil application or early crop establishment
dilution_dosage_per_acre.water_volume: NA; 25-50 kg compost carrier or drip water
dilution_dosage_per_acre.application_method: Soil incorporation, furrow placement, or drip-supported root-zone delivery

dilution_dosage_per_acre.crop_stage: Active vegetative stage or nutrient top-up
dilution_dosage_per_acre.application_method: Root-zone drench or drip application near feeder roots

application_repeatability.application_timing: At sowing, transplanting, or basal land preparation
application_repeatability.recommendation: Introduce the consortium early so rhizosphere colonization starts before nutrient demand peaks.

application_repeatability.application_timing: During active vegetative growth or after heavy leaching
application_repeatability.recommendation: Reapply to sustain microbial nutrient cycling when crop demand or nutrient loss remains high.

shelf_life_statement: Typical shelf life: 12 months in the unopened original pack under cool, dry, shaded storage.
compatibility_statement: Do not mix with antimicrobial dressers, strong fungicides, bactericides, or highly saline concentrates.

problem_solution.problem: Applied fertilizer is not translating into uniform crop nutrition.
problem_solution.solution: Use the consortium to improve nutrient conversion, cycling, and root-zone uptake efficiency.

problem_solution.problem: Soil biological activity is low and nutrient release is slow.
problem_solution.solution: Apply early to stimulate rhizosphere colonization and multi-nutrient mobilization.

storage_safety: Store sealed in the original pack away from direct sunlight, heat, and moisture.
technical_positioning_summary: Broad-spectrum consortium positioned as a base biological nutrient-efficiency input where N, P, and K response must be improved together.

product_overview: IGreen SHIELD is a rhizosphere-conditioning microbial consortium biofertilizer intended to stabilize nutrient capture under stressed or biologically weak soil conditions. It is positioned where root-zone activity, nutrient retention, and early crop establishment require reinforcement.
key_product_highlights: Rhizosphere-conditioning consortium for biologically weak soils
Supports nutrient solubilization, nitrogen contribution, and root-zone activity
Improves biological buffering around the active root system
Useful in transplant, basal, and stress-prone soil programs

mechanism_of_action.description: The consortium colonizes the rhizosphere, contributes associative nitrogen fixation, solubilizes native and applied nutrient pools, and sustains microbial nutrient cycling around feeder roots. By maintaining an active microbial zone near roots, it improves nutrient capture efficiency and stabilizes crop nutrition under stress-prone soil conditions.

problem_solution.problem: Soil biological activity is weak and transplanted crops establish slowly.
problem_solution.solution: Apply the consortium early to condition the rhizosphere and improve nutrient capture around roots.

problem_solution.problem: Nutrient efficiency drops under stress-prone or repeatedly disturbed soils.
problem_solution.solution: Use follow-up applications to sustain microbial cycling and root-zone nutrient buffering.

storage_safety: Store sealed in the original pack away from direct sunlight, heat, and moisture.
technical_positioning_summary: Rhizosphere-conditioning consortium positioned for stressed soils, transplant establishment, and stabilization of biological nutrient capture.

product_overview: IGreen N is a nitrogen-oriented microbial consortium biofertilizer intended to improve biological nitrogen supply and crop uptake efficiency. It is suited to systems where nitrogen-use efficiency and vegetative response need support beyond mineral fertilization alone.
key_product_highlights: Nitrogen-focused consortium with associative biological N contribution
Improves rhizosphere activity and plant access to available nitrogen
Supports greener canopy development and vegetative vigor
Useful where N loss or poor N efficiency is common

mechanism_of_action.description: The consortium colonizes the rhizosphere and root surface, fixes atmospheric nitrogen through associative diazotrophic members, and supports microbial nutrient cycling that improves nitrogen conversion and uptake. Supportive nutrient solubilization in the root zone further enhances the efficiency of applied fertilizer nitrogen.

problem_solution.solution: Use the nitrogen-focused consortium to improve biological N contribution and root uptake efficiency.

problem_solution.problem: Nitrogen losses reduce the return from applied fertilizers.
problem_solution.solution: Apply early and repeat if needed to strengthen rhizosphere cycling and nitrogen availability.

storage_safety: Store sealed in the original pack away from direct sunlight, heat, and moisture.
technical_positioning_summary: Nitrogen-oriented consortium positioned to improve biological N supply, canopy vigor, and total nitrogen-use efficiency.

product_overview: IGreen P is a phosphorus-oriented microbial consortium biofertilizer for improving phosphate solubilization and crop access to fixed soil P. It is best suited to soils where phosphorus is present but poorly available to roots.
key_product_highlights: Phosphorus-focused consortium for release of fixed soil phosphate
Improves root-zone P availability and fertilizer efficiency
Supports early root growth and energy-demanding crop stages
Useful in calcareous or P-fixing soils

mechanism_of_action.description: The consortium colonizes the rhizosphere and solubilizes insoluble phosphorus through organic acid production and allied microbial nutrient cycling processes. Supportive associative nitrogen fixation and root-zone colonization improve total nutrient capture, while released phosphate becomes more available for root uptake.

problem_solution.problem: Soil tests indicate phosphorus reserves but crop response remains weak.
problem_solution.solution: Apply the consortium to convert fixed phosphate into more plant-available forms.

problem_solution.problem: Root growth is slow in P-fixing or calcareous soils.
problem_solution.solution: Use as a basal and early vegetative input to improve rhizosphere P availability.

storage_safety: Store sealed in the original pack away from direct sunlight, heat, and moisture.
technical_positioning_summary: Phosphorus-solubilizing consortium positioned for low-P efficiency soils and stronger root establishment.

product_overview: IGreen K is a potassium-oriented microbial consortium biofertilizer designed to mobilize non-exchangeable K from soil minerals and improve crop K uptake. It is relevant where potassium response is limited by poor mineral weathering or low nutrient-use efficiency.
key_product_highlights: Potassium-mobilizing consortium for release of native soil K
Supports enzyme function, turgor balance, and crop quality
Improves K-use efficiency alongside overall rhizosphere activity
Useful in soils with high total K but low available K

mechanism_of_action.description: The consortium colonizes the rhizosphere, mobilizes potassium from mineral fractions through organic acid and chelation-mediated processes, and supports microbial nutrient cycling around roots. Supportive nitrogen-fixing and nutrient-solubilizing members improve total nutrient capture while increasing access to plant-available potassium.

problem_solution.problem: Fruit, tuber, or grain quality traits are constrained by poor K efficiency.

storage_safety: Store sealed in the original pack away from direct sunlight, heat, and moisture.
technical_positioning_summary: Potassium-mobilizing consortium positioned for soils where native K is present but poorly available to crops.

product_overview: IGreen Zn is a zinc-oriented microbial consortium biofertilizer for improving Zn solubilization and root access to micronutrient pools in soil. It is suited to Zn-deficient or high-pH soils where micronutrient availability is restricted.
key_product_highlights: Zinc-solubilizing consortium for improved micronutrient availability
Supports enzyme systems, auxin-linked growth, and chlorophyll function
Improves zinc-use efficiency under alkaline or deficient conditions
Useful in nursery, basal, and early crop establishment programs

mechanism_of_action.description: The consortium colonizes the rhizosphere and solubilizes zinc-bearing compounds through acidification, chelation, and active microbial nutrient cycling. Supportive nitrogen-fixing and nutrient-mobilizing members maintain root-zone activity, resulting in improved zinc availability and more efficient plant uptake.

product_overview: IGreen S is a sulfur-oriented microbial consortium biofertilizer intended to improve sulfur oxidation, mobilization, and uptake in the root zone. It is relevant where sulfur nutrition limits protein formation and efficient nitrogen utilization.
key_product_highlights: Sulfur-mobilizing consortium for improved sulfate availability
Supports amino acid formation and efficient nitrogen metabolism
Improves sulfur-use efficiency in low-availability soils
Useful where leaching or low organic matter limits S supply

mechanism_of_action.description: The consortium colonizes the rhizosphere and promotes sulfur oxidation or mobilization into plant-available sulfate forms while sustaining active microbial nutrient cycling around roots. Supportive nitrogen-fixing and nutrient-solubilizing members improve the overall efficiency of nutrient capture and utilization in the crop.

product_overview: IGreen Si is a silicon-oriented microbial consortium biofertilizer for mobilizing plant-available silicon from silicate minerals in the soil. It is suited to programs that aim to improve structural resilience and nutrient-use efficiency through better Si uptake.
key_product_highlights: Silicate-mobilizing consortium for improved plant-available silicon
Supports stronger cell wall function and abiotic stress buffering
Enhances rhizosphere mineral weathering and nutrient cycling
Useful in stress-prone systems where Si nutrition is beneficial

mechanism_of_action.description: The consortium colonizes the rhizosphere and mobilizes soluble silicon from silicate-bearing minerals through microbial weathering, acidification, and nutrient cycling processes. Supportive nitrogen-fixing and nutrient-solubilizing activity improves root-zone function, allowing better access to silicon and associated nutrients.
